kata.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
kata.workload: greedy O(n) max profit with unlimited transactions: profit += max(0, prices[i]-prices[i-1]) over N=2,000,000 LCG prices in [1,4096], K=10 outer calls, sum-of-results sink

env.note: Measured on a shared x86-64 Linux cloud container (karac from kara main), NOT the corpus's Apple M5 Pro (this kata's results.json is the M5 canonical). Absolute times/sizes/RSS NOT comparable across hosts; only within-file cross-language ratios are the signal. SHORT workload (~24-32ms), so read ratios not absolute ms. Seq lane (fastest first): c 24ms ~ rust 24ms < rust_ovf 29ms ~ kara 29ms < go 32ms. RESULT: kara is at EQUAL-SAFETY PARITY with rust_ovf (both ~29ms, ~1.20x behind wrapping rust -O / C). The ~1.20x vs rust -O is the OVERFLOW-CHECK tax, not a codegen gap: kara's greedy loop is `sub prices[i]-prices[i-1]; jo (overflow); jle (skip if <=0); add` with BOTH the forward index prices[i] AND the OFFSET index prices[i-1] bounds-check-elided (the `for i in 1..n` guard proves i-1>=0 and i<n) — only 1 panic site in the whole binary. All mirrors agree on the sink; correctness verified across interp/JIT/AOT/auto-par and vs a greedy==DP==brute-force ground truth. Python (K=10, timed separately) omitted. Re-run bench.sh on the M5 for the canonical table.
